metadata.title: Add async payment webhook ingestion pipeline
metadata.description: Introduces background worker queue for webhook events and dedup cache for retries.
metadata.author: senior-dev

hunks.content:
def ingest_webhook(request):
    signature = request.headers.get('X-Signature', '')
    payload = request.body.decode()
    if not verify_signature(signature):
        return {'ok': False}, 401
    event = json.loads(payload)
    enqueue_event(event['id'], payload)
    return {'ok': True}, 202

def process_event(event_id, payload):
    if redis.get(f'evt:{event_id}'):
        return
    redis.setex(f'evt:{event_id}', 60, '1')
    data = json.loads(payload)
    charge_id = data['charge_id']
    amount = data['amount']
    apply_balance_update(charge_id, amount)
    audit_log('processed', event_id)

additions: 56:     row = db.fetch_one('SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id=%s', (charge_id,))
58:     db.execute('UPDATE accounts SET balance=%s WHERE id=%s', (new_balance, charge_id))

hunks.content:
def apply_balance_update(charge_id, amount):
    row = db.fetch_one('SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id=%s', (charge_id,))
    new_balance = row['balance'] + amount
    db.execute('UPDATE accounts SET balance=%s WHERE id=%s', (new_balance, charge_id))

issues.description: Signature validation ignores payload and timestamp allowing replayed requests with stale signatures
Idempotency check and set are non-atomic, enabling duplicate event processing under concurrent workers
Dedup TTL of 60 seconds is too short for payment provider retries causing replay acceptance after expiration
Ledger update path lacks tenant/account ownership validation enabling cross-account balance manipulation
Read-modify-write balance update is not transactional and can lose increments under concurrent updates
Audit logging does not include processing latency or retry count, reducing incident forensics quality
